title: Blink Charging Q2 2025 Earnings Call Highlights
text: Blink Charging Co Q2 2025 Earnings Highlights. Financial Performance. Revenue: Total revenues reached $28.7 million, reflecting a 38% sequential increase from Q1 but declining from $33.3 million year-over-year. Product revenues jumped 73% sequentially to $14.5 million, while service revenue grew 46% annually to $11.8 million. Profitability Concerns: Gross profit deteriorated significantly to $2.1 million (7.3% margin) compared to $10.7 million (32% margin) in the prior year period. The company reported a net loss of $0.31 per share and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $24.4 million. Operational Metrics. Energy Distribution: Company achieved record performance delivering 49 gigawatt hours in Q2 2025, a 66% year-over-year increase, demonstrating substantial growth in charging volume. Cash Position: Cash reserves declined sharply to $25.3 million from $55 million at year-end 2024, with approximately $30 million burned in the first half of the year. Cost Management: Management implemented a 22% reduction in Q2 2025 compensation expenses and identified $16.5 million in non-cash charges including inventory write-offs and asset impairments. Strategic Developments: Zemetric acquisition addressed portfolio gaps for fleet and multifamily market segments, Envoy restructuring eliminated $23.5 million in contingent liabilities.
